zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Howard Justice Of Common Pleas於1240年出生在英國諾福爾可維根哈爾 聖 彼得時，他的父親Sir John Howard26歲，母親Lucia Germund22歲。他於1276年之前與Gilla de Terrington在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1308年5月3日在他的家鄉享年68歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可易亞斯特 文其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Howard Justice Of Common Pleas于1240年出生在英国諾福爾可維根哈爾 聖 彼得时，他的父亲Sir John Howard26岁，母亲Lucia Germund22岁。他于1276年之前与Gilla de Terrington在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1308年5月3日在他的家乡享年68岁，埋葬在英国諾福爾可易亞斯特 文其。

nameMeanings.name: Howard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, from the Middle English personal names Huward (also Howard) and Heward, from Old French Huard (itself from ancient Germanic Hugihard, hugi- ‘mind, understanding, spirit’ + hard- ‘hardy, bold’). As Hugh appears in Middle English as both How and Hew, this is the definite origin of Heward and a source of Howard. This surname is also very common among African Americans. See Hugh .

English: from the Middle English personal name Haward or Howard, usually an Anglicized form of Old Danish Hāwarth (Old Norse Hávarthr, from há ‘high’ + varthr ‘guard, guardian, warden’). Alternation between Haward and Howard may have led to later confusion with Hayward .
English: occasionally a variant of   Ewart   2.

variants.name: Fogarty
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, James Patrick, Kieran, Seamus, Siobhan.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Fógartaigh ‘son of Fógartach’, a personal name from fógartha ‘proclaimed, banished, outlawed’. It is sometimes Anglicized as Howard .

variants.name: Huggard
meanings: English: of Norman origin, from the medieval personal name Hugard, a variant of Huard (see Howard ). This surname is also established in Ireland (Kerry).

variants.name: Hayward
meanings: occupational name from Middle English hayward ‘keeper of the hedges and enclosures’ (Old English hēgweard), a manorial officer in charge of the common fields who protected corn and hay from cattle, theft, or damage, and supervised the harvest.

variants.name: Huard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aime, Adelard, Jacques, Jean-Pierre, Lucien, Marcel, Alcide, Amiee, Armand, Aurele, Clermont, Fernard.
French and Channel Islands (Jersey): from the Old French personal name Huart, either identical in origin with Howard 1 or a pet form of Old French Hue .
History: Jean Huard from Courson-Monteloup in Essonne, France, married Anne-Marie Amiot in Quebec City, QC, in 1670.

variants.name: Hugh
meanings: English, Welsh, and Scottish (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Hue, from ancient Germanic Hugo (ultimately derived from hugi- ‘mind, spirit’). This was a very popular name in England after the Norman Conquest, partly due to the reputation of Saint Hugh of Lincoln (1140–1200), who was born in Burgundy and who established the first Carthusian monastery in England.
Scottish and Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic personal name Aodh, a derivative of a Gaelic word meaning ‘fire’. See Hughes .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 邱 and 丘, based on their Hakka pronunciation; see   Qiu   1 and 2.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Howard Justice Of Common Pleas was born in 1240, in Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, England, his father, Sir John Howard, was 26 and his mother, Lucia Germund, was 22. He married Gilla de Terrington before 1276, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 3 May 1308, in his hometown, at the age of 68, and was buried in East Winch, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Howard Justice Of Common Pleas est né en 1240, à Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Howard, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Lucia Germund, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Gilla de Terrington avant 1276, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 3 mai 1308, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterré à East Winch, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William Howard Justice Of Common Pleas 1240 in Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Howard 26 und seine Mutter Lucia Germund 22. Er hat vor 1276 in England Gilla de Terrington geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 3. Mai 1308 im Alter von 68 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in East Winch, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Howard Justice Of Common Pleas è nato il nel 1240 nel Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, England, il suo padre, Sir John Howard, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Lucia Germund, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Gilla de Terrington prima del 1276, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 3 maggio 1308, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 68 anni, e fu sepolto a East Winch, Norfolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Howard Justice Of Common Pleasが1240年にイギリスノーフォルクウィグジェンハル スト ペターで生まれた時，父Sir John Howardは26歳，母Lucia Germundは22歳であった。1276年以前にイギリスでGilla de Terringtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1308年5月3日に故郷で68歳で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクエアスト ウィンチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Howard Justice Of Common Pleas(이)가 1240년 영국 노르폴크 위겐홀 스트 피터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Howard(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Lucia Germund(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1276년 이전 영국에서 Gilla de Terrington(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1308년 5월 3일 그의 고향에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 이스트 윈치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Howard Justice Of Common Pleas nasceu em 1240, em Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Howard, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Lucia Germund, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Gilla de Terrington antes 1276, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 3 de maio de 1308, em sua cidade natal, com 68 anos, e foi sepultado em East Winch, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Howard Justice Of Common Pleas родился в 1240 г. в местности вигенолл ст питир, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John Howard было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Lucia Germund, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Gilla de Terrington перед 1276 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 3 мая 1308 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 68 лет и был похоронен в местности эаст винч, норфолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William Howard Justice Of Common Pleas nació en 1240, en Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Howard, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Lucia Germund, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Gilla de Terrington antes de 1276, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 3 de mayo de 1308, en su pueblo, a la edad de 68 años, y fue sepultado en East Winch, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
